content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 28, 2014


David Edward Goddard
2118 Wilshire Blvd., # 419
Santa Monica, CA 90403

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-14-131

Dear Mr. Goddard:

This letter is in response to your request for advice dated July 16, 2014, on behalf of the Santa Monica Airport Commission and the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); Reg. 18329(a), copy enclosed.)) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Reg. 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; Reg. 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division and it would be inappropriate to advise at this time.

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice from you or private counsel.

_____________
¹. Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 – 18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought written advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act applicable to the Santa Monica Airport Commission.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same or similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, and providing advice would be inappropriate at this time.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (W-14-131W). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in the document. The question and conclusion are reconstructed from the body of the letter. Document clearly indicates FPPC's reasoning for declining to provide written advice is a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

QUESTION: The requestor sought written advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act applicable to the Santa Monica Airport Commission.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same or similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, and providing advice would be inappropriate at this time.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for written advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act for the Santa Monica Airport Commission due to pending related enforcement investigation. The declination letter notes that the decision is not a determination on the merits and that the official may seek legal advice from private counsel.
